title: INNER JOIN
instruction:
`INNER JOIN` returns rows where the join condition matches in both tables. Rows with no match are excluded.

Run this.
successMessage: Carol has no orders, so she doesn't appear. INNER JOIN = only rows with a match in BOTH tables.

title: LEFT JOIN — include all left rows
instruction:
`LEFT JOIN` returns all rows from the left table even when there's no matching row on the right. Missing values are `NULL`.

Run this.
successMessage: Carol has no orders but appears with `total = 0` (thanks to `COALESCE`). LEFT JOIN is great for 'show all X with optional Y' queries.

title: Multiple JOINs
instruction:
Chain multiple JOINs to bring together data from more than two tables.

Run this.
successMessage: Three tables, two JOINs. The query reads: 'give me each book with its author's name and sales figures, sorted by copies sold.'
